content:
Remote Scheduling Tools: Calendly vs Doodle vs Acuity Comprehensive Comparison

Overview

Calendar scheduling tools are essential for remote teams to coordinate meetings across time zones, eliminate scheduling friction, and maintain productive workflows. This comprehensive review compares three leading scheduling platforms: Calendly, Doodle, and Acuity Scheduling, analyzing their features, pricing, and suitability for remote team coordination.

Calendly: Automated Scheduling Leader

Calendly has become the standard for automated meeting scheduling, eliminating the back-and-forth emails that plague remote team coordination.

Key Features

  • Automated Booking: Shareable links for self-service scheduling
  • Calendar Sync: Integrates with Google, Outlook, iCloud, Office 365
  • Time Zone Intelligence: Automatic detection and display across time zones
  • Round Robin: Distribute meetings across team members automatically
  • Group Scheduling: Collective events with multiple attendees
  • Workflow Automation: Automated reminders, follow-ups, and confirmations

Pricing

  • Free Plan: 1 calendar, basic features, unlimited events
  • Standard: $10/user/month - 2 calendars, team scheduling
  • Professional: $15/user/month - 6 calendars, advanced workflows
  • Teams: $20/user/month - team features, admin controls
  • Enterprise: Custom pricing - SSO, advanced security, dedicated support

Best For

Teams needing automated scheduling with minimal friction. Ideal for sales teams, customer support, and any role requiring frequent external meetings.

Doodle: Group Poll-Based Scheduling

Doodle specializes in group scheduling through poll-based selection, making it ideal for finding consensus meeting times across distributed teams.

Key Features

  • Poll-Based Scheduling: Create polls for group time preferences
  • Calendar Connect: Sync with major calendar platforms
  • 1:1 Scheduling: Direct booking links similar to Calendly
  • Group Events: Schedule meetings with multiple participants easily
  • Time Zone Support: Automatic conversion for global teams
  • Video Integration: Built-in Zoom, Teams, Google Meet integration

Pricing

  • Free Plan: Basic polls, limited features
  • Pro: $14.95/month - unlimited polls, calendar sync, custom branding
  • Team: $19.95/user/month - team features, admin dashboard
  • Enterprise: Custom pricing - advanced features, dedicated support

Best For

Teams scheduling internal meetings where consensus matters. Excellent for team standups, cross-functional meetings, and any scenario requiring group input.

Acuity Scheduling: Appointment Management Platform

Acuity Scheduling offers robust appointment management with extensive customization, ideal for service-based businesses and consulting teams.

Key Features

  • Flexible Scheduling: Multiple appointment types with custom durations
  • Client Self-Booking: Branded booking pages for external clients
  • Payment Integration: Collect payments during booking (Stripe, PayPal)
  • Package Scheduling: Sell appointment packages and subscriptions
  • Intake Forms: Custom forms for client information collection
  • Calendar Sync: Multiple calendar platform integration

Pricing

  • Free Emerging: Basic features for freelancers
  • Growing Business: $23/month - calendars, intake forms, payments
  • Powerhouse Player: $44/month - advanced features, API access
  • Enterprise: Custom pricing - white label, dedicated support

Best For

Service-based remote teams requiring payment collection and client management. Ideal for consultants, coaches, therapists, and any role with paid appointments.

Comparison Summary

FeatureCalendlyDoodleAcuity
Scheduling MethodAutomated linksPoll-basedAppointment types
Time Zone SupportAutomaticAutomaticAutomatic
Payment IntegrationBasic (Premium)NoFull (Stripe, PayPal)
Starting Price$10/user/month$14.95/month$23/month
Best Use Case1:1 meetingsGroup schedulingPaid appointments
Team FeaturesExcellentGoodLimited

Selection Recommendations

Choose Calendly if:

  • You need automated 1:1 scheduling with minimal friction
  • Your team schedules frequent external client meetings
  • Round robin distribution across team members is required

Choose Doodle if:

  • Group scheduling with consensus is your primary need
  • You schedule internal team meetings frequently
  • Poll-based selection fits your workflow

Choose Acuity if:

  • You collect payments for appointments or consultations
  • You need custom intake forms for client information
  • Package scheduling and subscriptions are relevant to your business

Key Takeaways

When selecting remote scheduling tools:

  1. Match to scheduling type: Calendly for 1:1, Doodle for groups, Acuity for paid
  2. Consider time zones: All three handle global teams well
  3. Evaluate integration needs: Check calendar and video platform compatibility
  4. Test with stakeholders: Free tiers allow evaluation before commitment
  5. Plan for growth: Choose scalable solutions as team expands
